title: lantana triplinervia var. puberulenta
intro: Lantana triplinervia var. puberulenta, also known as small shrub lantana, is a variety of shrub native to Mexico and Central America, characterized by its small, fragrant flowers and woody stems. The leaves and stems of this plant have been used in traditional medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antifungal properties. The health benefits of lantana triplinervia var. puberulenta include its ability to treat respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate symptoms of colds and flu. Lantana triplinervia var. puberulenta can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or decoction, and its leaves and stems can also be used in salves and ointments for topical application.
